data.title: Dynex Capital, Inc. corrects dividend amount for Q3, 2025 on Series C Preferred Stock to $0.64147. Payable on Oct 15, 2025.
data.description: In Glen Allen, Virginia, Dynex Capital, Inc. (NYSE: DX) issued a correction regarding the third quarter 2025 cash dividend for its outstanding shares of Series C Fixed-to-Floating Rate Cumulative Redeemable Preferred Stock (NYSE: DXPRC). The corrected dividend amount is $0.64147 per share and will be paid on October 15, 2025. Shareholders of record as of October 1, 2025, will be eligible to receive the dividend. The correction was made for the dividend originally declared on September 11, 2025. Additionally, the company made an announcement related to another matter.

data.title: Global Medical REIT Inc. finishes one-for-five reverse stock split, every 5 shares consolidated into 1.
data.description: Global Medical REIT Inc. (NYSE: GMRE) based in Bethesda, Maryland, has recently finished executing a one-for-five reverse stock split. This action applied to all issued, outstanding, and authorized shares of their common stock with a par value of $0.001 per share. The reverse stock split came into effect at 5:00 p.m. Eastern Time on the announced day. Following the split, shareholders now hold one share for every five they previously owned.

data.title: Mogo seeks to extend expiry date of 77,778 warrants issued to Postmedia Networks Inc. as part of marketing agreement.
data.description: In Vancouver, British Columbia, Mogo Inc. (NASDAQ:MOGO) (TSX:MOGO) has made a request to the Toronto Stock Exchange to prolong the expiry date of 77,778 common share purchase warrants given to Postmedia Networks Inc. These warrants were issued under a marketing collaboration deal and are originally due to expire on September 20, 20XX. Mogo, a company specializing in digital wealth, payments, and Bitcoin services, is seeking to extend the duration of these warrants.

data.title: Revive Therapeutics closes second tranche of private placement, issuing 4,352,381 units at $0.021 each for $91,400.
data.description: In Toronto on September 19, 2025, a specialty life sciences company named Revive Therapeutics Ltd. revealed the closure of a second portion of its private placement funding. This involved issuing 4,352,381 units priced at $0.021 each, resulting in total earnings of $91,400 for Revive. The company, traded under symbols OTCQB: RVVTF, CSE: RVV, and FRANKFURT: 31R, focuses on creating innovative therapeutics to address critical medical needs. This announcement followed the initial mention of the private placement on September 5, 2025.

data.title: Partners Value Split Corp. to redeem all 5,996,800 Class AA Preferred Shares, Series 9 on Oct. 6, 2025.
data.description: On September 19, 2025, Partners Value Split Corp. revealed plans to redeem all 5,996,800 of its outstanding Class AA Preferred Shares, Series 9 on October 6, 2025. The redemption will be done in cash in line with the terms of the Preferred Shares, Series 9.

data.title: LGI Homes announces opening of new community Shelm Meadows near Olympia, WA in Yelm, Washington.
data.description: LGI Homes is excited to introduce the Grand Opening of Shelm Meadows, a fresh residential development situated in Yelm, Washington.

data.title: Iovance Biotherapeutics granted 22,810 stock options to 2 new employees on Sep 18, 2025 under NASDAQ Rule 5635(c)(4).
data.description: On September 18, 2025, in San Carlos, California, Iovance Biotherapeutics, Inc. (NASDAQ: IOVA), revealed that they granted 22,810 shares of Iovance’s common stock as inducement stock options to two new non-executive employees. Iovance, a biotech company emphasizing on developing innovative polyclonal tumor infiltrating lymphocyte (“TIL”) therapies for cancer patients, made this announcement as part of their efforts to expand their workforce.

data.title: Nofar USA secures 1 GW of solar projects, adding to 1.2 GWh storage portfolio, expanding operations by 2026-2029.
data.description: Nofar USA, a subsidiary of Nofar Energy, based in Fort Lauderdale, Florida, has successfully acquired more than 1 gigawatt (GW) of solar development projects in diverse U.S. markets. This achievement was made possible through a strategic combination of acquisitions and exclusive development agreements. The deals were finalized towards the end of August and significantly augment Nofar USA's existing portfolio. The projects consist of both utility-scale and distributed generation schemes, with operational commencement slated between 2026 and 2029. These important acquisitions signify a significant step for Nofar USA in the renewables sector.
